title: helianthemum cinereum subsp. argenteum
intro: Helianthemum cinereum subsp. argenteum, also known as Rockrose, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as having antioxidant properties that can protect against cell damage. The Rockrose plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also sometimes used as a food ingredient or ingredient in cosmetics due to its purported health benefits and antimicrobial properties.
